content: The difference in how intrinsic reward scaling between ICM/RND and canonical curious agents reveals an unrecognized principle: curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (CRL) methods’ intrinsic reward generation inherently acts as a built-in active memory pruning signal for active learning agents, where underperforming CRL variants naturally filter out low-information redundant memory entries that active learning would otherwise waste capacity storing.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
